title: Add Algolia Instant Search to a Next.js project
summary: Complete Algolia setup — create index, push records, InstantSearch React UI, facets, SSR, and common pitfalls.
declaration: I will integrate Algolia into this project: create an index, configure environment variables, push records, and build a live search UI with InstantSearch React. If you prefer Meilisearch, Typesense, or Postgres full-text search, say so before I begin.
instructions:
## Add Algolia Instant Search to Next.js ### 1. Create an Algolia account and index - Sign up at https://www.algolia.com (free tier: 10,000 records, 10,000 search ops/month). - In the dashboard → Indices → **New index**. Name it (e.g., `products`, `articles`, `posts`). - Note your **Application ID**, **Search-Only API Key** (public), and **Admin API Key** (server-only, secret). ### 2. Install packages - Install the search client and UI library: ``` npm install algoliasearch react-instantsearch ``` - Optional — for a rich autocomplete input: ``` npm install @algolia/autocomplete-js @algolia/autocomplete-preset-algolia ``` ### 3. Configure environment variables - Add to `.env.local`: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_APP_ID=YOUR_APP_ID NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_SEARCH_KEY=YOUR_SEARCH_ONLY_API_KEY NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_INDEX_NAME=your_index_name ALGOLIA_ADMIN_KEY=YOUR_ADMIN_API_KEY ``` - `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix exposes a variable to the browser — only use it for the Search-Only key, App ID, and index name. - NEVER expose the Admin API Key to the client. It has full write access to your index. ### 4. Push records to the index (server-side script) - Create `scripts/algolia-index.ts`: ``` import algoliasearch from 'algoliasearch'; const client = algoliasearch( process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_APP_ID!, process.env.ALGOLIA_ADMIN_KEY! ); const index = client.initIndex(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_INDEX_NAME!); const records = [ { objectID: '1', name: 'Blue T-Shirt', category: 'Clothing', price: 29.99 }, { objectID: '2', name: 'Running Shoes', category: 'Footwear', price: 89.99 }, ]; index.saveObjects(records).then(({ objectIDs }) => { console.log('Indexed:', objectIDs); }); ``` - `objectID` is required — it uniquely identifies each record. Without it Algolia assigns random IDs and duplicates pile up on re-indexing. - Run with: `npx ts-node --skip-project scripts/algolia-index.ts`. - For production, call `index.saveObjects()` from a webhook, cron job, or server action whenever your data changes. ### 5. Create the search client (client-safe) - Create `src/lib/algolia.ts`: ``` import algoliasearch from 'algoliasearch'; export const searchClient = algoliasearch( process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_APP_ID!, process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_SEARCH_KEY! ); ``` - This uses the Search-Only key — safe to import in Client Components. ### 6. Build the InstantSearch UI - Create `src/components/Search.tsx`: ``` 'use client'; import { InstantSearch, SearchBox, Hits, Highlight, Configure } from 'react-instantsearch'; import { searchClient } from '@/lib/algolia'; function Hit({ hit }: { hit: any }) { return (

{hit.category} — ${hit.price}

); } export function Search() { return ( ); } ``` - `` renders matched text with highlights — always use it for good search UX. - `` sets query parameters like `hitsPerPage`, `filters`, and `facetFilters`. ### 7. Add InstantSearch CSS - Install and import the default theme: ``` npm install instantsearch.css ``` - In `app/globals.css`: ``` @import 'instantsearch.css/themes/satellite-min.css'; ``` - Or style manually using the `.ais-SearchBox`, `.ais-Hits`, `.ais-Highlight` class names. ### 8. Configure searchable attributes and ranking - In Algolia dashboard → Index → **Configuration** → **Searchable attributes**: list the fields to match (e.g., `name`, `category`, `description`). - Or set programmatically (ideal for version-controlled config): ``` index.setSettings({ searchableAttributes: ['name', 'category', 'description'], customRanking: ['desc(popularity)'], attributesForFaceting: ['category', 'price'], }); ``` - Run this once (or on deploy) to keep index config in source control. ### 9. Faceted filtering (optional) - Add a category filter sidebar: ``` import { RefinementList } from 'react-instantsearch'; ``` - The attribute must appear in `attributesForFaceting` in index settings — otherwise refinements silently return nothing. ### 10. Verify integration - Start the dev server: `npm run dev`. - Type in the search box — hits should appear and update in real time. - Check the Algolia dashboard → Index → **Browse** to confirm records are present. - Inspect network requests: calls should go to `*.algolia.net` using the Search-Only key. ### Common errors & fixes - **'ApplicationID is required'** → `NEXT_PUBLIC_ALGOLIA_APP_ID` not set. Restart the dev server after editing `.env.local`. - **'Invalid API key'** → Using the Admin Key as the Search Key, or keys from the wrong application. Check the Algolia dashboard. - **Hits are empty after indexing** → Records lack `objectID`, or `indexName` in `` doesn't match the index. Verify in the dashboard → Browse. - **No styles on search UI** → Missing InstantSearch CSS import. Add to global CSS. - **'Index does not exist'** → `indexName` doesn't match. Index names are case-sensitive. - **TypeScript error on `hit` prop** → Type your hit: `import type { Hit } from 'instantsearch.js'; function Hit({ hit }: { hit: Hit })`. - **Stale results after data update** → Re-run the indexing script or trigger `index.saveObjects()` from your data pipeline. - **Admin Key exposed in client bundle** → You imported the admin client in a Client Component. Admin operations must be server-only. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT use the Admin API Key in the browser — it gives anyone full write access to your index. - Do NOT skip setting `objectID` — Algolia will create duplicate records on every re-index run. - Do NOT call `index.clearObjects()` before every re-index in production — it creates a gap where search returns no results. Use `index.saveObjects()` for partial updates or atomic re-indexing via a temp index. - Do NOT put all content in a single searchable attribute with no ranking — configure `searchableAttributes` and `customRanking` for relevant results. - Do NOT use Algolia for database-style filtering on admin dashboards — it costs search operations. Use your DB for admin queries. - Do NOT hardcode API keys in source code — always use environment variables. - Do NOT skip `attributesForFaceting` configuration when using `` — it silently returns no refinements.
